concerns: In PasswordUtil, the passwords are hashed using plain SHA-256 without any unique per-user salt or slow-hashing derivation function (like BCrypt or PBKDF2), making them highly susceptible to precomputed rainbow table attacks.

In BookmarkController, tag matching is performed in-memory rather than at the database/repository query level. This will result in high memory consumption and poor query performance as the bookmark dataset grows.

The JwtUtil class performs manual JWT string manipulation and custom Base64Url parsing/claims extraction instead of using standard production-ready JWT libraries (e.g., jjwt or java-jwt), which can introduce security edge cases, validation gaps, or parse bugs.
